# Define normalization
import re
arabic_tashkeel = ''.join([
    '\u0610','\u0611','\u0612','\u0613','\u0614','\u0615','\u0616','\u0617','\u0618','\u0619','\u061A',
    '\u064B','\u064C','\u064D','\u064E','\u064F','\u0650','\u0651','\u0652','\u0653','\u0654','\u0655','\u0656','\u0657','\u0658','\u0659','\u065A','\u065B','\u065C','\u065D','\u065E','\u065F',
    '\u0670','\u0671','\u06D6','\u06D7','\u06D8','\u06D9','\u06DA','\u06DB','\u06DC','\u06DF','\u06E0','\u06E1','\u06E2','\u06E3','\u06E4','\u06E7','\u06E8','\u06EA','\u06EB','\u06EC','\u06ED'
])
arabic_tashkeel_re = re.compile("[{}]+".format(''.join(arabic_tashkeel)))

def normalize_ar(text):
    # remove tashkeel
    txt = re.sub(arabic_tashkeel_re, "", text)
    # remove tatweel
    txt = txt.replace('\u0640','')
    # unify hamza forms: map different hamzas to bare alif? instruction: unify hamza.
    # We'll map: 'أ','إ','آ','ٱ' to 'ا' and 'ء' remains?
    txt = txt.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا').replace('ٱ','ا')
    # remove punctuation and non letters/digits
    # Keep Arabic letters and numbers and spaces. Replace other punctuation with space.
    txt = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF0-9\s]', ' ', txt)
    # collapse whitespace and lower-case (though Arabic has no case)
    txt = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', txt).strip()
    return txt

# test normalize
query = "وَمَن يُشِرْك بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ حَرَّمَ الْخِطَابَ الْحَسَنَ وَأَوْفَى بِعَهْدِهِ"
print(query)
print(normalize_ar(query))

len(qdata)

query_norm = normalize_ar(query)

len(matches), matches[:5]

from difflib import SequenceMatcher
def token_stats(a,b):
    atoks=a.split()
    btoks=b.split()
    a_set=set(atoks)
    b_set=set(btoks)
    common=a_set & b_set
    prec = len(common)/len(a_set) if len(a_set)>0 else 0.0
    rec = len(common)/len(b_set) if len(b_set)>0 else 0.0
    if prec+rec==0:
        f1=0.0
    else:
        f1=2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)
    return {'prec':prec,'rec':rec,'f1':f1,'common':common,'len_common':len(common)}

def lcs_ratio(a,b):
    # normalized LCS length over max length
    sm=SequenceMatcher(None,a,b)
    lcs_len = sum(triple.size for triple in sm.get_matching_blocks())
    # Alternatively, SequenceMatcher ratio gives similar metric
    ratio = sm.ratio()
    return {'lcs_chars':lcs_len, 'seq_ratio':ratio}

# Compute scores for all qdata items
scores = []
for item in qdata:
    a = query_norm
    b = item['norm']
    t = token_stats(a,b)
    s = lcs_ratio(a,b)
    # combine metrics into a composite score: weighted avg
    composite = 0.6*t['f1'] + 0.4*s['seq_ratio']
    scores.append((composite, t['f1'], s['seq_ratio'], item))
# sort descending
scores_sorted = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
top = scores_sorted[:20]
[(round(c,4), round(f1,4), round(seq,4), it['surah_name'], it['ayah_id'], it['ayah_text']) for c,f1,seq,it in top[:10]]

# gather top candidates across both quran and hadiths using composite score
def score_against_query(text):
    a = query_norm
    b = normalize_ar(text)
    t = token_stats(a,b)
    s = lcs_ratio(a,b)
    composite = 0.6*t['f1'] + 0.4*s['seq_ratio']
    return composite, t['f1'], s['seq_ratio']

cands = []
for item in qdata:
    sc = score_against_query(item['ayah_text'])
    cands.append((sc, 'q', item))
for item in hdata:
    txt = get_hadith_texts(item)
    if not txt: continue
    sc = score_against_query(txt)
    cands.append((sc, 'h', item))
# sort

top20 = cands_sorted[:20]
